name: Digital Identity Guidelines — Enrollment and Identity Proofing
owner: National Institute of Standards and Technology
description: Identity assurance levels and identity-proofing requirements. Companion to 800-63B (Authentication) and 800-63C (Federation). NIST has since published the 800-63-4 series (2024) which supersedes the 800-63-3 series.
relationToCloudSecurity: Federal digital-identity assurance baseline for cloud-hosted federal information systems and federated cloud services.
relationToAISecurity: Applies to AI systems handling identity proofing, authentication, or federation within scope.

license.notes: Work of NIST is in the US public domain (17 USC §105).

lifecycle.notes: SP 800-63-3 series remains the most widely-cited federal digital-identity baseline through transition to 800-63-4 (2024).

current_extraction.notes: Source PDF marker-extracted to markdown. extract_800_63a.py walks the numbered-section structure into per-section structured CSV/JSON across 10 chapters (Purpose, Introduction, Definitions, Identity Assurance Level Requirements, Identity Resolution/Validation/Verification Requirements, Derived Credentials, Threats, Privacy, Usability, References).
